Author.title: Author
Author.description: Represents an author who writes blog posts for the FashionGirl blog.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Author entity.

name.description: The full name of the author.

bio.description: A short biography or description of the author.

profileImageUrl.description: URL to the author's profile picture.

Category.title: Category
Category.description: Represents a category used to organize blog posts and products.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Category entity.

slug.description: A URL-friendly slug for the category, used for routing.

description.description: A brief description of the category's content or theme.

imageUrl.description: URL to an image representing the category, used in featured sections.

BlogPost.title: BlogPost
BlogPost.description: Represents an individual article published on the FashionGirl blog.

id.description: Unique identifier for the BlogPost entity.

title.description: The main title of the blog post.

slug.description: A URL-friendly slug for the blog post, used for routing.

excerpt.description: A short summary or preview of the blog post content.

content.description: The full body content of the blog post, potentially in HTML or Markdown format.

coverImageUrl.description: URL to the main cover image for the blog post.

publishDate.description: The date and time when the blog post was published.

readingTimeMinutes.description: Estimated reading time of the blog post in minutes.

isFeatured.description: Indicates if the blog post is highlighted as a featured article.

authorId.description: Reference to the Author of this blog post. (Relationship: Author 1:N BlogPost)

categoryIds.description: References to Categories associated with this blog post. (Relationship: Category N:N BlogPost)

Product.title: Product
Product.description: Represents an individual fashion item available in the FashionGirl catalog.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Product entity.

name.description: The name of the product (e.g., 'Vestido Floral Midi').

description.description: A detailed description of the product.

imageUrl.description: URL to the main image of the product.

price.description: The selling price of the product.

sku.description: Stock Keeping Unit, a unique identifier for product variations.

isAvailable.description: Indicates if the product is currently in stock and available for purchase.

categoryIds.description: References to Categories that this product belongs to. (Relationship: Category N:N Product)

CarouselSlide.title: CarouselSlide
CarouselSlide.description: Represents a single slide in the main hero carousel on the homepage.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CarouselSlide entity.

title.description: The title text displayed on the carousel slide.

imageUrl.description: URL to the background image for the carousel slide.

linkUrl.description: The URL the carousel slide navigates to when clicked.

order.description: The display order of the slide within the carousel.

definition.description: Stores information about blog post authors. Publicly readable by all users, writeable only by authenticated administrators.
Manages categories for blog posts and products. Publicly readable by all users, writeable only by authenticated administrators.

Contains individual blog articles. Publicly readable by all users. Writeable only by authenticated administrators. Includes denormalized 'authorId' and 'categoryIds' for efficient display and querying.

Stores details of fashion products for the catalog. Publicly readable by all users. Writeable only by authenticated administrators. Includes denormalized 'categoryIds' for efficient display and querying.
Manages individual slides for the homepage hero carousel. Publicly readable by all users, writeable only by authenticated administrators.

Defines users with administrative access to the CRM. Access is determined by the existence of a document with the user's UID. Publicly readable by authenticated administrators only. Writeable only by existing authenticated administrators, ensuring Authorization Independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the administrator.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for the FashionGirl blog prioritizes **Authorization Independence** and **Structural Segregation** to ensure simple, robust, and debuggable security rules, especially for the `/painel` (CRM) functionality. All public-facing content entities (Authors, Categories, Blog Posts, Products, Carousel Slides) are stored in top-level collections, designed for global read access by all users, both authenticated and unauthenticated. This ensures that `list` operations (QAPs) on these collections are performant and secure, as rules do not need to filter results; all content is intended to be publicly viewable.

For administrative access to the CRM (`/painel`), a dedicated collection, `/roles_admin/{userId}`, is established. This collection implements a **Database Access Control (DBAC)** model, where the mere *existence* of a user's `uid` as a document ID in this collection grants them administrative privileges. This design is critical for **Authorization Independence** because checking a user's role involves a simple, atomic `exists(/databases/$(database)/documents/roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid))` call in security rules. This check does not depend on reading attributes from a parent document, thus avoiding the pitfalls of hierarchical authorization dependencies and allowing for atomic creation/management operations.

Furthermore, this structure adheres to **Structural Segregation** by clearly separating public data from authorization data. All documents within `/blog_posts`, `/products`, `/authors`, `/categories`, and `/carousel_slides` share a homogeneous security posture (public read, admin write), making security rules straightforward. Denormalization of `authorId` and `categoryIds` within `BlogPost` and `Product` documents simplifies data retrieval, but more importantly, since write access is globally restricted to admins, there's no complex per-document owner-based write authorization required that would necessitate further denormalization for individual authors.
